$comment: c48 (D-CSUBSET-BOOL-CHAR-WIDENING): a comparison/logical result (typed Bool) assigned to a plain `char` lhs. Frontier: sqlite3.c:25926 `p->nFloor = (p->D==31);` (nFloor is char) fired S0003 S_TypeMismatch — the isAssignable boolWidensToArith arm (type_rules.hpp) admitted Bool into the int/float RANK kinds but not plain `char` (TypeKind::Char, outside the ranks), so `char = (a==b)` (Bool) was rejected while `char = (a-b)` (int, in rank) was accepted via the charConvertsToArith arm. c48 adds Char to the Bool arm (gated on charConvertsToArith). NO codegen change: coerce() already lowers Bool->Char (a same-width Bitcast/mov — DSS Bool is 8-bit, not i1 — preserving the canonical 0/1). a=b=41 (kept runtime via io()): c=(a==b)=1, c+=(a<b)=+0 (still 1), d=(a&&b)=1, e=a-b=0 (the int->char differentiator, always OK) -> c+d+e+40 = 1+1+0+40 = 42. Covers comparison(==) + relational(<) + logical(&&) -> char, all assign-stmt (the sqlite shape). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the `lk == TypeKind::Char` term -> `c=(a==b)` fails S0003 (does not compile); `e=a-b` stays fine, proving the gap was the Bool-RESULT->char path, not int->char narrowing.
